title: When selecting everything from a joined result, column contents are incorrect in one of my use cases.
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE test2 (a INT, b VARCHAR(10), c FLOAT, d DATE);
INSERT INTO test2 (a, b, c, d) VALUES (1, 'test1', 1.1, '2023-01-01');
INSERT INTO test2 (a, b, c, d) VALUES (2, 'test2', 2.2, '2023-01-02');
SELECT sub1.a, sub1.b, sub1.c, sub2.d FROM (SELECT a, b, c FROM test2) AS sub1 JOIN (SELECT a, c FROM test2) AS sub2 ON sub1.a = sub2.a;
SELECT sub1.a, sub1.b, sub1.c, sub2.d FROM (SELECT a, b, c FROM test2 WHERE c IS NOT NULL) AS sub1 LEFT JOIN (SELECT a, d FROM test2 WHERE d IS NULL) AS sub2 ON sub1.a = sub2.a;
key_bug_features: Incorrect column contents in joined result selection
